Britt Evangelist, of Swanson & McNamara, Tel. 415.477.3800, for Shane White as executor.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT SHANE WHITE Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is GRANTED as to the 2nd cause of action, with 60 days leave to amend, as to malicious prosecution cause of action. The Court finds that it does not sufficiently allege favorable termination. The Court GRANTS causes of action 3-6, with 60 days leave to amend, for defamation claims. While the Court partially agrees that it is time barred, it is consistent with public policy in California at this early stage. The Court GRANTS the 7th cause of action for fraudulent conveyance claims. The Court finds that the claims fails as a matter of law because of lack of standing, as conceded. Defendants shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff David Replogle telephone number 209-274-5246 appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.
Katie Machado of Doll, Amir & Eley, LLP telephone number 310-557-9100 for Defendants Amazon.com and On-Demand Publishing.

The matter was discussed. Upon conclusion, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling in its entirety. Defendants On-Demand Publishing, LLC's and Amazon.com, Inc.'s demurrer to the third through sixth causes of action in the first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend as to all four causes of action. The moving parties' request for judicial notice is granted. The one-year statute of limitations of CCP 340(c) bars the third and fourth causes of action. The two-year statute of limitations of CCP 335.1 bars the fifth and sixth causes of action. Plaintiff's claims accrued on April 30, 2013, when defendants published the book that forms the basis of all of plaintiff's action. (Shively v. Bozanich (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1230, 1246; FAC 8:10-18.) Plaintiff filed this case on February 22, 2016. Plaintiff's tolling allegations regarding CCP 352.1 do not render his claims timely. The judicially noticeable documents show that plaintiff is serving a sentence of life without the possibility of parole. Under these circumstances, the tolling provisions of section 352.1 are inapplicable. (Brooks v. Mercy Hospital (2016) 1 Cal.App.5th 1, 3.) Order signed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Aug-31-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - DAVID K. REPLOGLE, TEL. NO. 1-209-274-5246, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. J. PAT FERRARIS, TEL. NO. 1-951-530-3710 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT LISA DI MARIA.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Lisa DiMaria's motion to change venue is denied. The amended complaint at paragraph 8 alleges that defendant Shane White resides in San Francisco and his declaration supports that allegation. Accordingly, Ms. DiMaira fails to show that plaintiff commenced this action in the wrong court. "It is well-established in California law that '[a] defendant is not entitled to have an action removed to the county of his residence unless it appears that none of the other defendants are residents of the county where the action is brought.'" (K.R.L. Partnership v. Sup. Crt. (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 490, 503.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR # 6576 =302/hk
